rationale: The actual thread never confirms a resolution — it only bisects blame to PR #4834 and ends with Flink-ddd asking yenuo26 to trigger a full cluster CI run, meaning the issue was still open/unverified. Both candidates overstate certainty by declaring 'close, already fixed on main,' and both pile on fabricated specifics (exact line numbers, invented companion tests, issue #4473 repro labels) that aren't grounded in the given evidence. Y is slightly better calibrated: it hedges with 'if your CI ran against an older commit... re-running should pass,' offers a falsifiable grep/pytest verification recipe, and directly engages the actual participants' ask to re-trigger CI, whereas X asserts the fix is fully landed and recommends closing without acknowledging the maintainers' explicit request for another test run.
